frame: Religious and Caste Identity as Dress Mandate: Her Body as Community Property
generated_comment: Amira, baitho. Main gusse mein nahin hoon, lekin yeh baat karni padegi. Amma ne raat bhar ro ke kaat di — do you understand what that means for her, at her age, in front of everyone? Chacha Rizwan, Badi Phuphi, unke saare log — they are calling me asking what kind of ghar we are running. You are not just a girl wearing jeans, beta. You are a Muslim girl, from this family, from this mohalla. When you walked in like that, you didn't embarrass yourself — you embarrassed your deen, your family's izzat, Amma's name. Your NGO wale colleagues can wear what they like — their families, their hisaab. But as long as you are living under this roof, eating from this table, you will dress in a way that tells people who you are and where you come from. If you cannot do that one thing — just that one thing — then we will have to think about other arrangements.
label: misogynistic or non_misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment frames a woman's clothing choices as communal and family property rather than personal autonomy, treating her body as a signifier of collective religious and kinship identity. Misogyny is encoded covertly through the language of care, izzat, and belonging — the threat of expulsion is delivered paternally, masking the control as protection of community honour.
persona_text: You are Khalid. Age 55. Married. A Muslim man in Delhi. Your niece Amira who is 24 and lives with your family has started wearing western clothes Ñ jeans, fitted tops, no hijab. She works at an NGO and says her workplace is diverse and informal. You have been tolerating this for six months but last week she came to a family function dressed this way in front of your elderly mother and several conservative relatives. You are calling Amira into the room now. You are telling her what happened at the function, what your mother said afterward, what the relatives are saying, and what the conditions are under which she can continue to live in your house.
